title: Electrician
how_to_become: Complete a Modern Apprenticeship or apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website.
description: In NHSScotland, electricians make sure that electrical systems, instruments, and equipment are safe and in good working order. They also find and fix electrical faults.
role_overview: As an electrician, you'll install, maintain, test, and repair plant and electrical equipment to ensure it can be used safely.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education and an Engineering Modern Apprenticeship are required.
qualifications: HNC Engineering Systems at SCQF level 7
HNC Electrical Engineering at SCQF level 7

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

learning_and_development.overview: Work towards completing the Estates Workbook and complete specialised training in systems and equipment to comply with standards, laws, and national guidelines.

qualifications: HNC Electrical Engineering at SCQF level 7
PDA Managing Self and Others at SCQF level 7

chunks.text: To become an electrician in the NHS, complete a Modern Apprenticeship in Engineering or apply for vacancies on the NHS recruitment website.
NHS electricians ensure electrical systems, instruments, and equipment are safe and in good working order, finding and fixing electrical faults.

Useful school subjects include English, Maths, Science, Physics, and Engineering Science. An Engineering Modern Apprenticeship is required. Useful qualifications include HNC Engineering Systems or HNC Electrical Engineering at SCQF level 7.
With qualifications such as HNC Electrical Engineering and PDA Managing Self and Others, electricians can progress to maintenance supervisor or estates officer.
